title: The Building Bible: The Research System Behind Six-Figure Agents
dek: Ask a top rental agent about any luxury building in their market and watch what happens: pet policy, parking cost, which floor plans face west, how fast the office processes applications, the leasing manager's name. That's not memory. That's a system. I call mine the Building Bible.
heroAlt: The Building Bible: The Research System Behind Six-Figure Agents

blocks.html: One spreadsheet, one row per building, and it is the single most valuable document in my business. Core columns: building name, neighborhood, management company, leasing contacts, unit mix, price ranges, pet policy, parking, amenities, co-op policy, application requirements, and the column that matters most: a running log of every interaction I've had with that building.

blocks.text: The Building Bible beats the CRM you never open.

blocks.html: Speed: when a relocating executive emails "2 bed, under $5,000, walkable to the Loop, dog friendly," I answer with three precise options in minutes, not a day of research. Authority: on tours, knowing the building cold reads as competence, and competence is what luxury clients are actually shopping for. Relationships: interaction notes mean every conversation with a manager picks up where the last one left off, even months later.

One afternoon for the skeleton: fifty buildings, basic data from public sources. Then it grows forever: every tour, every call, every application teaches you something, and it goes in the row. Six months in, you own an asset no competitor can copy, because it's made of your accumulated attention.

A CRM tracks people, who move and change. The Bible tracks buildings, which stay. Renters churn every year or two; the building on the corner will still be leasing units when you're ten years in. Research that compounds is what separates a business from a hustle.
Template and walkthrough are in my free ebook Luxury Leases, Real Profits. Better agents win.
